calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Granted Per Written Order

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (409 Myra Way, San Francisco, Ca 94127)
Off Calendar Dec-10-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Executor).

Ms. Rasch stated that the Referee felt that the appraised value of the property was appropriate and was to remain. Mr. Le (Current Bidder) stated that he understood that the purchase amount did not meet 90% of the appraised value and that he was not interested in bidding a higher amount. The sale was confirmed at $517,500.00. The Court granted the petition, (subject to a written order). The amount of the additional bond is to be determined.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-03-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Proposed Executor).

Ms. Rasch stated that Mr. Brunn would not be appearing today and that Ms. Dolch's consent had been filed. The Court will appoint Debra Dolch as the Executor, (subject to a written order).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Nici Rodich, CSR#11604. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-11-2008 Continued To Sep-08-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for Linda McGovern. Charles Brunn for Robert Seagrave. Amy Harrington for Linda Harrington.

Mr. Brunn stated that his client denied all allegations, that the house was vacant and in shambles and that it was the intention of his client to preserve the assets. He also stated that he was prepared to do a complete accounting. Ms. Boesseecker stated that it was her client's position to have an independent administrator appointed and that she strongly objected to Robert Seagrave being appointed. Ms. Harrington stated that her client was willing to serve. After meeting outside, the parties returned and stated that they agreed to having Ms. Dolch be appointed. The petition will be amended. The matter was continued to September 08, 2008 for filing of the amended petition.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.
